basics.label: Lead Backend Engineer
basics.name: Raúl Correia
basics.summary: Lead Backend Engineer with 6+ years in software engineering and 10+ years of overall professional experience. Hands-on in AWS-native, event-driven backend services and API delivery with end-to-end ownership. Strong in requirements discovery, workflow automation, CI/CD, observability, and service migrations. Experienced in TypeScript, C#/.NET, and Go.

work.summary: Led Asset Management services on an AWS serverless stack. Key technologies: TypeScript, API design, Datadog, CloudWatch.
highlights: Led AWS serverless Asset Management services that unified multiple Shell Recharge APIs behind a single entry point for consumer teams
Led a five-person team including two senior engineers, one junior engineer, and one contractor
Owned API design and technical direction, from requirements shaping to delivery alignment with enterprise security standards
Partnered with product and stakeholders to align roadmap, requirements, and delivery
Contributed to the cross-team golden path for TypeScript and AWS services to improve maintainability and consistency
Implemented monitoring and alerting with Datadog and CloudWatch to improve reliability and observability
Coached and mentored engineers on serverless architecture, code quality, and delivery practices

work.summary: Business API / Bridge team. Key technologies: TypeScript, Node.js, AWS, SST, Serverless Framework, GitLab CI, Terraform, Docker, Vault, Datadog.
highlights: Delivered the Work Orders Service integrating Salesforce with Shell/Shell Recharge systems to keep charge point repairs available and protect EV uptime
Maintained >99.9% uptime across multiple services, including Work Orders
Built and maintained Node.js/TypeScript services using SST and Serverless Framework
Operated AWS services including DynamoDB, S3, and CloudWatch with Datadog monitoring
Delivered CI/CD in GitLab and infrastructure automation with Terraform and Docker
Reduced end-to-end pipeline time by 30% across multiple projects
Managed secrets and configuration with HashiCorp Vault and aligned delivery with cross-discipline stakeholders

work.summary: Backend engineer for a multi-tenant KYC/KYB platform in banking that achieved SOC 2 Type I certification. Key technologies: AWS Lambda, DynamoDB, TypeScript/Node.js, Serverless Framework, AWS CDK v2, KYC/KYB, CloudWatch/X-Ray.
highlights: Designed a dynamic onboarding backend enabling configurable customer onboarding workflows across clients
Integrated KYC/KYB and fraud checks with multiple vendor APIs to strengthen compliance and security
Built serverless services on AWS Lambda and DynamoDB with TypeScript and Node.js
Migrated infrastructure from Serverless Framework to AWS CDK v2 to reduce technical debt and enable dynamic multi-customer deployments from a single codebase
Built a declarative testing framework to capture dynamic customer workflows per tenant and deployment
Contributed to onboarding workflows that supported successful SOC 2 Type I certification
Improved observability with CloudWatch and X-Ray for production troubleshooting

work.summary: Backend developer for the Ostate architectural parametric generation platform. Key technologies: C#, C++, Python, AWS, Docker, Microservices.
highlights: Re-architected the platform to enable new generation requirements and algorithms
Implemented backend services and algorithms in C#, C++, and Python for building generation
Built AWS and Docker compute pipelines with a focus on performance and scalability

work.summary: Developer on Web team modernization efforts. Key technologies: JavaScript, C++, Java, ProcScript, GitLab CI/Jenkins, Linux/Windows.
highlights: Researched and prototyped modern web technologies for Uniface Web solutions
Implemented features across the low-code platform and C++ backend
Maintained cross-platform compatibility for Linux and Windows environments
Worked with GitLab CI/CD and Jenkins in a mixed Linux/Windows toolchain

work.position: Junior Software Developer - Maia, Porto, Portugal
work.summary: Full stack developer for Manufacturing Execution System (MES). Key technologies: C#/.NET, SQL Server, Angular, PowerShell, Selenium, Scrum.
highlights: Developed MES product features using C# .NET, SQL Server, and Angular
Worked in a Scrum team with requirements discussions and feature demos
Implemented MES features meeting ISO 9001 requirements, achieving 100% audit pass
Built scripts and automation with PowerShell and Selenium for quality and tooling

work.summary: Internship developing a .NET SDK for payment terminals. Key technologies: C#/.NET, TCP, TLS/OpenSSL, HTTP, multithreading.
highlights: Built a C# framework for terminal communication over TCP, TLS/OpenSSL, and HTTP
Implemented multi-threaded, event-oriented processing for payment events
Developed error handling and protocol support for reliable transactions
Contributed to SDK documentation and developer guidance

education.institution: Instituto Superior de Engenharia do Porto

awards.title: Gamegune 2008 Counter-Strike 1.6 World Tournament
awards.summary: Competed at the world tournament Gamegune in Counter-Strike 1.6.

awards.title: Competitive gaming rankings
awards.summary: CS:GO Global Elite (top 0.1%), Valorant Immortal (top ~1%), Dota 2 Divine (top ~5%).

projects.name: Career Wrapped
projects.description: Built and shipped over the March 21-22, 2026 AMS GenAI & Video Hackathon weekend: turns a public LinkedIn profile into a shareable career recap page and optional short MP4 using generative text plus image/video pipelines.

projects.name: theme-browser.nvim
projects.description: Built an open source Neovim theme browser with registry-driven preview, install, apply, and persistence flows; reached ~1.7k clones in the first day after launch.

projects.name: Movimento tech4COVID19 (Volunteer)
projects.description: Built a pharmacy queue module to support medicine ordering for COVID-positive elderly homes during the national emergency, collaborating on GDPR considerations and UI/UX clarity.

projects.name: Codecrafters HTTP Server in Go
projects.description: Developed an HTTP server from scratch in Go as part of the Codecrafters challenge, focusing on low-level networking and protocol implementation.

projects.name: Lambda Component Testing Framework
projects.description: Created a testing framework in TypeScript/Node.js for AWS Lambda functions, enabling direct testing of deployed Lambdas across teams.
